file_insights.insight: New or significantly refactored shared color constants module for CLI UI components
Modified to extract and reuse color constants from shared colors module instead of defining locally
Updated to use shared color constants, indicating coordination with gantt.rs extraction
Modified to import and expose colors module for use across CLI subcommands
Related spec tracking color and stats CLI UX improvements, synchronized with this extraction work

patterns_discovered.pattern: DRY principle applied to color constants
patterns_discovered.description: Color definitions centralized from multiple files (gantt.rs, list.rs) into single shared colors.rs module
patterns_discovered.significance: Reduces maintenance burden and ensures UI consistency

patterns_discovered.pattern: Module extraction followed by cross-component refactoring
patterns_discovered.description: After colors.rs extraction, gantt.rs and list.rs both updated to import and use shared constants

patterns_discovered.pattern: Metadata-driven spec tracking with build commits
patterns_discovered.description: Commit a0fa83e tracked in build_commits.json for subtask-1-1, enabling session history reconstruction
patterns_discovered.significance: Supports reproducible builds and rollback capability

patterns_discovered.description: Spec 017 (quicklook-zellij-links) and spec 018 (cli-ux-colors-stats) share color constant concerns
patterns_discovered.significance: Indicates proper dependency coordination in wave-based execution

gotchas_discovered.issue: Color constant scoping across multiple crates
gotchas_discovered.description: Must ensure colors.rs is accessible to both gantt and list modules without circular dependencies
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use standard Rust module visibility (pub const) with proper path imports

gotchas_discovered.issue: Color definition consistency between CLI and macOS Quick Look
gotchas_discovered.description: Spec 017 involves Swift Quick Look colors while 018 involves Rust CLI colors; must maintain consistency
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document color palette in shared specification or configuration file

gotchas_discovered.issue: Limited diff visibility in session data
gotchas_discovered.description: Actual code changes truncated in git diff; cannot verify completeness of refactoring
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Refer to commit a0fa83e directly for full implementation details

approach_outcome.description: Extract color constants from gantt.rs to shared colors module or reuse directly
approach_outcome.key_achievement: Centralized color definitions into single authoritative module (colors.rs), eliminating duplication across gantt.rs and list.rs

recommendations.recommendation: Document color constant naming convention and palette usage guidelines in README or CONTRIBUTING.md
recommendations.rationale: Ensures future contributors maintain consistency when adding new colors

recommendations.recommendation: Create a shared color palette specification (JSON or TOML) that can be used across both Rust CLI and Swift macOS components
recommendations.rationale: Spec 017 (Swift) and 018 (Rust CLI) both need consistent colors; shared spec prevents divergence

recommendations.recommendation: Add unit tests to colors.rs validating color values match expected RGB/hex codes
recommendations.rationale: Prevents accidental color mutations and ensures visual consistency over time

recommendations.recommendation: Consider generating color constants from a centralized source-of-truth (e.g., design system JSON) using code generation
recommendations.rationale: Scales approach to handle more components and design changes without manual refactoring

recommendations.recommendation: Review other CLI modules (e.g., dashboard, metrics) for similar color constant duplication
recommendations.rationale: Expand this refactoring pattern to achieve consistent color handling across all CLI output
